text: We are still living from an informational standpoint in a pre-AI state of mind, where all the products are still focused on storage and capture - all they're doing is moving words from one place you don't read them to another place you don't read them.
context: V's thesis on why AI productivity feels like a scam - existing tools optimize for capture, not actionability.

text: AI productivity feels like a scam because they certainly don't feel more productive. If anything, they feel more overloaded.
context: Articulating the widespread founder frustration with AI tools based on conversations with top VCs and super-connectors.

text: If Ben's talk ends on a note of freedom and control, and like the joys of living in your own apartment - I can touch on that note of vulnerability about how I've traveled so much, lived in so many places, and my room was always very bare.
context: V preparing the emotional arc for the Zo demo - connecting digital ownership to physical home-making as a metaphor.

text: It's not about a single workflow. It's about the Gestalt, right? It's about how all the workflows - it's the totality of your data.
context: Articulating the key insight about Zo's value proposition - holistic data control vs. isolated automations.

text: The big unlock for founder productivity has been having better control over my information - where it goes, where it flows, what state it ends up, and then how you re-leverage it.
context: Summarizing V's personal productivity thesis for the Zo demo.

text: It's like up there with huge unlocks in my life, like the first time I used ChatGPT or the first time I used LimeWire.
context: Comparing Zo's impact to paradigm-shifting technology moments - brackets it with both cutting-edge AI and nostalgic P2P.

text: Productivity is the freedom to have more of my time, more of my ownership over my decisions, less time spent feeling like I'm being reactive and more time being proactive.
context: V's definition of productivity framed as agency and control rather than output metrics.

text: I never knew what I had been missing. Being able to not being constrained to just the limits of what Ikea, a landlord and a minimal amount of effort could put together.
context: V using home furnishing as metaphor for pre-Zo productivity limitations - generic tools vs. personalized systems.
